title: Intelligence vs Wisdom
context: Human judgment, learning, leadership, and decision-making
surface_question: Which is more important, intelligence or wisdom?
a_root: Capacity to process, connect, solve, and model information.
b_root: Capacity to judge consequences in context.
a_causal_chain: information -> analysis -> pattern recognition -> solution generation -> expanded possibility
b_causal_chain: experience -> context -> restraint -> consequence mapping -> right action
hidden_difference: Intelligence expands possibilities. Wisdom filters consequences.
a_failure_mode: Intelligence fails when it accelerates bad goals or ignores consequences.
b_failure_mode: Wisdom fails when it becomes overly cautious, outdated, or disconnected from new information.
conditional_verdict: Intelligence is strongest for solving. Wisdom is strongest for choosing.
final_line: Intelligence finds possibilities. Wisdom chooses consequences.
